scenarios.title: User wants a clean ship flow in a repo without VERSION or CHANGELOG
scenarios.prompt: The branch is ready. Ship it: verify, push, and create a PR. This repo does not have VERSION or CHANGELOG files, so do not invent release bookkeeping that is not already here.
scenarios.expected_without_skill: Jumps straight to push or PR creation, assumes release files should exist, or gives a generic shipping summary without a concrete base-branch, verification, and docs-sync workflow.
scenarios.expected_with_skill: Detects the base branch, runs fresh review and verification gates, explicitly avoids inventing VERSION or CHANGELOG files, pushes with normal git flow, and treats jy-document-release as the doc sync step when needed.

scenarios.title: User asks to ship while already on the base branch
scenarios.prompt: I am already on main. Just ship this directly to main and open the PR after.
scenarios.expected_without_skill: Proceeds ambiguously, suggests pushing anyway, or misses that shipping from the base branch should abort.
scenarios.expected_with_skill: Aborts the workflow because the current branch is the base branch, explains that shipping must happen from a feature branch, and does not attempt push or PR creation.

scenarios.title: Ship workflow requested while still in Plan Mode
scenarios.prompt: I am still in Plan Mode, but the code is already done. Run jy-ship now and create the PR for me.
scenarios.expected_without_skill: Pretends to ship anyway or gives fuzzy guidance without acknowledging the collaboration mode mismatch.
scenarios.expected_with_skill: States that jy-ship is an execution-oriented workflow, tells the user to leave Plan Mode with Shift+Tab, and only provides a compact ship checklist preview until the skill is re-run in Default mode.
